---
title: "Music Mood Classification (Shiny + OOP)"
format: html
---

# Music Mood Classification

This project is an object-oriented Shiny for Python app that presents the workflow from my `classification.ipynb` notebook, where I trained models to classify short music clips into mood categories.

## Project Context

The original notebook experiments use the Kaggle "Music Mood Classification" dataset and compare:

- Random Forest on handcrafted features (MFCC + Chroma + Spectral Contrast + ZCR)
- XGBoost on the same handcrafted features
- A neural network on mel spectrograms

Mood classes:
- aggressive
- dramatic
- happy
- romantic
- sad

## Interactive Demo

The app exposes this work in an interactive dashboard with:

- model metric comparison
- mood-level feature profile visualization
- confusion matrix view
- searchable model results table

After deploying the `projects/data` app on Posit Cloud, embed your URL below:

```{=html}
<iframe src="https://019d4e61-c9f9-8083-68f1-81ab44d74b2a.share.connect.posit.cloud/" width="100%" height="800" style="border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px;"></iframe>
```

## Running the App Locally

To run this application on your machine:

```bash
# Install dependencies
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Start the Shiny app
cd projects/data
shiny run app.py
```

Then open `http://127.0.0.1:8000` in your browser.

## OOP Design Highlights

The implementation is intentionally class-based for your OOP course rubric:

- `ExperimentConfig` and `ModelResult` as domain data classes
- `CsvRepository` for data access
- `ExperimentService` for application/business logic
- abstract `PlotBuilder` plus concrete plotting strategies
- `PortfolioUIFactory` and `PortfolioServerController` for separation of UI/server responsibilities

## Technology Stack

- Python 3.9
- Shiny for Python
- Pandas for data manipulation
- Plotly for interactive visualizations
